claim: The text-only section's elevated entropy (3.9016 bits, highest of all sections) reflects an underlying plaintext that has undergone minimal or no transposition â€” i.e., it is a pure substitution cipher or near-plaintext â€” whereas sections with lower entropy (zodiac 3.7149, biological 3.7977) involve additional transposition steps that reduce apparent glyph entropy by increasing local repetition.
evidence_for: Text-only section has highest entropy (3.9016 bits), approaching natural Latin/Italian levels (~3.95-4.0)
Transposition ciphers applied to natural language typically reduce local bigram entropy by redistributing glyph sequences
Existing high-confidence belief (0.956) suggests text-only section represents closest approximation to natural language statistics
evidence_against: Lower entropy in other sections could reflect semantic content differences (e.g., zodiac labels are inherently less entropic) rather than transposition
Biological section entropy (3.7977) may reflect illustration-driven repetition of botanical/anatomical terms rather than transposition
Only 7 folios in text-only section limits statistical power
test: For each section, compute the excess entropy (or multi-information) at lag-1 and lag-2: I_k = H(g) - H(g_i | g_{i-1}, ..., g_{i-k}); compare these values across text-only, herbal, biological, and zodiac sections; a section with transposition will show relatively lower excess entropy at short lags due to disrupted local correlations.
test_metric: Lag-1 and lag-2 conditional entropy for each section; excess entropy relative to unigram entropy
test_threshold: Confirm transposition hypothesis if text-only section shows significantly higher lag-1 excess entropy than zodiac and biological sections (difference > 0.1 bits); deny if all sections show comparable excess entropy profiles

reasoning: Differentiating sections by their local correlation structure (not just unigram entropy) would identify which sections use transposition and which use pure substitution, enabling section-specific attack strategies and directly testing whether entropy variation is cipher-driven or content-driven.
